evidence.evidence_text: The compressive strength of the full water cured concretes is included in Table 7. The 28-day compressive strength of the OPS concretes varies between around 43 and 48 MPa.
The optimum percentage limestone filler (by weight of cement, replaced by fine aggregate) of 20% and the highest value of compressive strength of 48.33 MPa were obtained in this study.
Eight different OPS concrete mixtures were produced; mixes 3-5 used partial replacement limestone powder (10, 20 and 30% of OPC by weight) by the fine aggregate.

review_queue.question: Confirm the mix ID and complete age series from Table 7, which is referenced but not visible in the packet.

main_risks: Some candidate compressive-strength data are figure-only, truncated, or missing mixture/age/value mapping in the supplied packet.
